hypothesis: If models are retrained on Kekulized GEOM-Drugs data using chemically accurate valency representations, then the generated molecular geometries will exhibit smaller distortions (lower Δr, Δθ, Δφ) and lower relaxation energy ΔE_relax when evaluated with GFN2-xTB compared to models trained on original data, because chemically correct valency and unambiguous aromatic representation reduce geometry errors relative to QM-optimized references.
